title: Sanger Sequencing Preparation and Submission
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"23s4j","text":"This protocol describes the preparation and submission process for Sanger sequencing. steps: Thaw the PicoGreen dye reagent at room temperature in a place protected from light. Ensure that the PicoGreen dye reagent is wrapped in aluminum foil, as it is photosensitive.
Prepare 4X PicoGreen (dsDNA quant solution).
Label a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube with "4X Pico". Wrap this microcentrifuge tube in aluminum foil.
Prepare 0.5 µL of 1X TAE buffer and molecular-grade water. To do this, add 25 µL of PicoGreen kit TAE (20X) and 475 µL of molecular-grade water to the labeled microcentrifuge tube.
Add 10 µL of fully thawed PicoGreen dye to the labeled microcentrifuge tube. This is not exactly 4X, but it does not matter.
Add 40 µL of your 4X Pico to one whole tube of Q5 high-fidelity master mix New England Biolabs ( 1250 mL ).

For each isolate, label: 1) 200 µL PCR tube for initial lysis (Tube Set 1) 2) 200 µL PCR tube for dilution (Tube Set 2) Additionally, obtain and label the required amount of 96-well PCR plates. The amount will vary based on the number of your samples. Include tubes for a positive control (previously isolated genomic DNA) and a negative control. You do not need to label a lysis tube for the positive control.
Add 125 µL of molecular-grade water to Tube Set 1 for boiling lysis.
Add culture.
If using a solid colony, add a loopful of colonies into the water. Use a sterile inoculating loop for the negative control.
If using a liquid culture, add 50 µL of the vortexed culture to the water. For the negative control, add 50 µL of molecular-grade water.
Vortex to mix.
Load Tube Set 1 into a thermocycler. Use the parameters: 1) 99 °C for 00:15:00 2) 4 °C for temporary storage
Move to a -20 °C freezer for 00:05:00 .
8000 rpm, °C, 5 minutes .
Dilute newly obtained lysate in Tube Set 2.
Add 18 µL of molecular-grade water.
Add 2 µL of your spun-down sample. Be careful to avoid disturbing the cell pellet.
Add 2 µL of previously isolated genomic DNA for the positive control.
To prepare PCR reactions, use the attached spreadsheet. Add the calculated quantities to your 96-well plate(s). This spreadsheet uses the primer pair 27F-1492R.
Spin down PCR samples in a plate spinner.
Load into qPCR machine. Use the program: 1) 98 °C for 00:03:00 2) 30 cycles: 1. 94 °C for 00:01:00 2. 55 °C for 00:01:00 3. 72 °C for 00:00:45 3) 72 °C for 00:15:00 4) 4 °C for temporary storage
Follow the attached protocol from Qiagen.
Visit: https://eurofinsgenomics.com/en/products/dna-sequencing/sanger-sequencing/
Follow the steps listed for sample submission.
creator: Raven Lewis
